prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
# Chronicle of the Quarterly Reckoning, Year of Our Lord 2024

Verily, I have been tasked to record that the Lord of this Digital Manor hath increased his monthly recurring tribute by seventeen score ducats, though I confess I know not whether this coin be real or merely phantom numbers inscribed upon parchment by sorcerous calculating-engines. The vassals do speak in tongues most strange of "churn rates" and "customer acquisition costs," words that sound as though they concern the butter-making peasants, yet the ledgers suggest these are measures of faith—for who shall remain loyal to this invisible service they cannot hold in their hands? In mine bewilderment, I attempted to sketch the "dashboard" they presented, only to realize it contained more squares and colorful sigils than the very heavens themselves, and yet the merchants seemed most pleased by its appearance. I am told the company must now pursue the distant lands of "enterprise clients" ere the coffers run dry, much as a knight must venture beyond his castle walls, though I suspect the danger here comes not from dragons but from something far more treacherous: the shareholders.
